name: Menu
description: A dropdown menu triggered by a button, supporting items, checkbox items, radio items, submenus, and keyboard navigation. Built on Base UI Menu with StyleX styling.

Root.description: Provides menu state and context. Does not render a DOM element.

onOpenChange.description: Callback when the menu open state changes.

Trigger.description: The button that opens the menu. Handles aria-expanded, aria-haspopup, and keyboard shortcuts.

dataAttributes.data-popup-open: Present when the menu popup is open.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the trigger is disabled.

Popup.description: The menu popup container. Contains the menu items.

dataAttributes.data-open: Present when the menu is open.
dataAttributes.data-closed: Present when the menu is closed.
dataAttributes.data-starting-style: Present on first render when opening for CSS transition.
dataAttributes.data-ending-style: Present when closing for CSS transition.

Item.description: A menu item. Supports keyboard navigation and focus management.

destructive.description: Whether the item represents a destructive action, styled with destructive colors.

closeOnClick.description: Whether clicking the item closes the menu.

dataAttributes.data-highlighted: Present when the item is highlighted via keyboard or pointer.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the item is disabled.

Separator.description: A visual separator between menu items.

cssRequirements.description: Menu popup enter/exit animations using Base UI data attributes.

intents.reasoning: Menu is the primary component for dropdown menus triggered by a button. It provides keyboard navigation, focus management, and ARIA attributes.
Menu supports checkbox items for toggling boolean options within a menu.
Menu supports nested submenus for organizing complex menu hierarchies.

avoidWhen.scenario: Navigation links that should be visible at all times
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use a visible navigation bar or NavigationMenu instead. Menus hide their content until triggered.
avoidWhen.alternative: NavigationMenu or plain nav with links

avoidWhen.scenario: Selecting a single value from a list (form input)
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Select or Combobox for form value selection. Menu is for actions, not form values.
Use ContextMenu which handles the right-click trigger pattern. Menu requires an explicit trigger button.

examples.name: with-links
examples.description: Menu with link items for navigation
